name: Japanese N1 Grammar
description: Master the ~200 advanced Japanese grammar patterns tested at JLPT N1 — covering concession and contrast, formal written register, literary expressions, complex conditionals, emphasis, temporal sequences, and nuanced stance markers. Fill in the blank to prove you can deploy each pattern precisely in context.

facts.quizQuestion:
緊張の{___}、声が震えてしまった。
(Because of too much nervousness, my voice trembled.)
facts.explanation: あまり (because of excess/too much) — 緊張のあまり、声が震えてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kinchou no ___ , koe ga furue te shima ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Because of too much nervousness, my voice trembled.
facts.grammarPointLabel: あまり — because of excess/too much

facts.quizQuestion:
嬉しさの{___}、思わず涙が出てきた。
(Because of too much joy, tears naturally welled up.)
facts.explanation: あまり (because of excess/too much) — 嬉しさのあまり、思わず涙が出てきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ureshi sa no ___ , omowazu namida ga de te ki ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Because of too much joy, tears naturally welled up.
facts.grammarPointLabel: あまり — because of excess/too much

facts.quizQuestion:
驚きの{___}、彼は言葉を失った。
(Because of too much surprise, he was left speechless.)
facts.explanation: あまり (because of excess/too much) — 驚きのあまり、彼は言葉を失った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: odoroki no ___ , kare ha kotoba wo ushina ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Because of too much surprise, he was left speechless.

facts.quizQuestion:
心配する{___}、夜も眠れなくなってしまった。

facts.sentenceTranslation: Because of worrying too much, I became unable to sleep at night.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}暑さに、外出する気にもなれなかった。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ atsu sa ni, gaishutsu suru ki ni mo nare naka ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Because of the excessive heat, I couldn't even bring myself to go outside.
facts.grammarPointLabel: あまりの～に — because of too much ~

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}痛さに、思わず叫んでしまった。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ ita sa ni, omowazu saken de shima ta

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}美しさに、しばらく言葉が出なかった。

facts.quizQuestion:

sentenceFurigana.g: to come off (of a button, handle, lid, etc.)

facts.quizQuestion:
厳しく言う{___}、本当に期待しているのだ。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kibishiku iu ___ , hontouni kitai shi te iru no da
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is precisely because I care that I speak harshly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばこそ — precisely because

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to take (seat, position, course, office, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: doryoku sure ___ , kekka ga tsui te kuru toiu mono da
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is precisely because one makes effort that results follow.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばこそ — precisely because

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたのことを思え{___}、こんなに心配しているのだ。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: anata no koto wo omoe ___ , konnani shinpai shi te iru no da
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is precisely because I think about you that I worry so much.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばこそ — precisely because

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: shinken ni torikume ___ , kore hodo no seika ga de ta no da

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kuni wo mamora ___ , ooku no heishi ga inochi wo sasage ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: For the purpose of protecting the country, many soldiers gave their lives.

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: shinjitsu wo akiraka ni se ___ , kare ha naganen niwatatte chousa wo tsuzuke ta

facts.grammarPointLabel: んがために — for the purpose of/literary

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kokorozashi wo hatasa ___ , kare ha kannanshinku ni tae ta

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
景気が悪い{___}、求職者数は増加の一途をたどっている。
(Also because the economy is bad, the number of job seekers continues to increase.)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: keiki ga warui ___ , kyuushoku sha suu ha zouka no itto wo tado te iru

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga yuumeijin toiu ___ , sono hatsugen ha sugu ni seken no chuumoku wo atsume ta

facts.quizQuestion:
不慣れな{___}、ご迷惑をおかけすることもあるかと存じますが、よろしくお願い申し上げます。
(Since I am unaccustomed, I believe there may be times I cause you inconvenience, but I humbly ask for your understanding.)
facts.explanation: こととて (because/since — archaic/formal) — 不慣れなこととて、ご迷惑をおかけすることもあるかと存じますが、よろしくお願い申し上げます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: funare na ___ , go meiwaku wo okake suru koto mo aru ka to zonji masu ga, yoroshiku onegai moushiage masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since I am unaccustomed, I believe there may be times I cause you inconvenience, but I humbly ask for your understanding.
facts.grammarPointLabel: こととて — because/since — archaic/formal

facts.quizQuestion:
子どもの{___}、何も知らずに失礼なことを申したかもしれません。
(Since it was a child, they may have said something rude without knowing.)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kodomo no ___ , nani mo shira zu ni shitsurei na koto wo moushi ta kamo shire mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since it was a child, they may have said something rude without knowing.
facts.grammarPointLabel: こととて — because/since — archaic/formal

facts.quizQuestion:
初めての{___}、至らない点が多々ございましたことをお詫び申し上げます。
(Since it was my first time, I sincerely apologize for the many shortcomings.)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: hajimete no ___ , itara nai ten ga tata gozai mashi ta koto wo owabi moushiage masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since it was my first time, I sincerely apologize for the many shortcomings.
facts.grammarPointLabel: こととて — because/since — archaic/formal

facts.quizQuestion:
若気の至りという{___}、当時の無礼をどうかお許しください。

facts.explanation: こととて (because/since — archaic/formal) — 若気の至りということとて、当時の無礼をどうかお許しください。

facts.quizQuestion:
部下の{___}、上司として弱音を吐くわけにはいかない。
(Because I am in the position of a superior in front of my subordinates, I cannot show weakness.)

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceTranslation: Because I had bragged so much, I couldn't admit that I had failed.

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.sentenceTranslation: Because I made a promise, I cannot give up no matter how hard it gets.

facts.quizQuestion:
お客様の{___}、社員として恥ずかしい行動はとれない。

sentenceFurigana.g: to come off (of a button, handle, lid, etc.)

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
久しぶりの帰郷{___}、親戚が大勢集まってきた。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarPointLabel: 限りは — as long as

facts.quizQuestion:
私が生きている{___}、あなたを守ります。
(As long as I am alive, I will protect you.)
facts.explanation: 限りは (as long as) — 私が生きている限りは、あなたを守ります。The action continues for the full duration of the condition.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi ga iki te iru ___ , anata wo mamori masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: As long as I am alive, I will protect you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 限りは — as long as

facts.quizQuestion:
ルールに違反しない{___}、何をしても構いません。
(As long as you don't violate the rules, you may do anything.)
facts.explanation: 限りは (as long as) — ルールに違反しない限りは、何をしても構いません。Permits broad freedom within a stated boundary.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ruuru ni ihan shi nai ___ , nani wo shi te mo kamai mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: As long as you don't violate the rules, you may do anything.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 限りは — as long as

facts.quizQuestion:
体が動く{___}、この仕事を続けたい。
(As long as my body can move, I want to continue this work.)
facts.explanation: 限りは (as long as) — 体が動く限りは、この仕事を続けたい。Expresses committed intention tied to physical capability.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: karada ga ugoku ___ , kono shigoto wo tsuzuke tai
facts.sentenceTranslation: As long as my body can move, I want to continue this work.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 限りは — as long as

facts.quizQuestion:
あの人に頭を下げる{___}、仕事を辞めたほうがましだ。
(Rather than bowing my head to that person, I'd rather quit my job.)
facts.explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — あの人に頭を下げるくらいなら、仕事を辞めたほうがましだ. The speaker finds the alternative more acceptable than an undesirable action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ano hito ni atama wo sageru ___ , shigoto wo yame ta hou ga mashi da
facts.sentenceTranslation: Rather than bowing my head to that person, I'd rather quit my job.
facts.grammarPointLabel: くらいなら — rather than

facts.quizQuestion:
嘘をつく{___}、何も言わないほうがいい。
(Rather than telling a lie, it is better to say nothing.)
facts.explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — 嘘をつくくらいなら、何も言わないほうがいい. Chooses silence as the lesser of two evils.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to take (seat, position, course, office, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: uso wo tsuku ___ , nani mo iwa nai hou ga ii
facts.sentenceTranslation: Rather than telling a lie, it is better to say nothing.
facts.grammarPointLabel: くらいなら — rather than

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな仕事を続ける{___}、転職したほうが幸せだ。
(Rather than continuing such a job, changing careers would make you happier.)
facts.explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — そんな仕事を続けるくらいなら、転職したほうが幸せだ. Recommends a drastic alternative over enduring a bad situation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sonna shigoto wo tsuzukeru ___ , tenshoku shi ta hou ga shiawase da
facts.sentenceTranslation: Rather than continuing such a job, changing careers would make you happier.
facts.grammarPointLabel: くらいなら — rather than

facts.quizQuestion:
我慢して食べる{___}、食べないほうがましだ。
(Rather than eating it while enduring it, it is better not to eat it at all.)
facts.explanation: くらいなら (rather than) — 我慢して食べるくらいなら、食べないほうがましだ. Rejects a joyless experience in favour of abstention.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: gaman shi te taberu ___ , tabe nai hou ga mashi da
facts.sentenceTranslation: Rather than eating it while enduring it, it is better not to eat it at all.
facts.grammarPointLabel: くらいなら — rather than

facts.quizQuestion:
もし時間を戻せる{___}、あの日に戻りたい。
(If I could turn back time, I would want to return to that day.)
facts.explanation: ものなら (if it were possible/hypothetical) — 時間を戻せるものなら、あの日に戻りたい。Expresses an impossible or unlikely wish.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: moshi jikan wo modoseru ___ , ano hi ni modori tai
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I could turn back time, I would want to return to that day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものなら — if it were possible/hypothetical

facts.quizQuestion:
逃げられる{___}逃げてみろ。
(If you can escape, just try to escape.)
facts.explanation: ものなら (if you dare/if it's possible — challenge) — 逃げられるものなら逃げてみろ。Used as a dare implying the action is impossible or will have consequences.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: nige rareru ___ nige te miro
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you can escape, just try to escape.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものなら — if you dare/if it's possible — challenge

facts.quizQuestion:
やり直せる{___}、もう一度挑戦したい。
(If I could start over, I would want to try once more.)
facts.explanation: ものなら (if possible/hypothetical) — やり直せるものなら、もう一度挑戦したい。Desire attached to an unrealistic or regrettable hypothetical.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: yarinaoseru ___ , mouichido chousen shi tai
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I could start over, I would want to try once more.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものなら — if possible/hypothetical

facts.quizQuestion:
文句を言える{___}、言ってみろ。
(If you can complain, just try saying it.)
facts.explanation: ものなら (if you dare) — 文句を言えるものなら、言ってみろ。A challenge implying the speaker doubts the listener will dare.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: monku wo ieru ___ , i te miro
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you can complain, just try saying it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ものなら — if you dare

facts.quizQuestion:
連絡が{___}、こちらから動くことはできない。
(Unless there is contact, we cannot take action from our side.)
facts.explanation: ない限り (as long as ... does not / unless) — 連絡がない限り、こちらから動くことはできない. Negative condition that must be met to allow the outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: renraku ga ___ , kochira kara ugoku koto ha deki nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Unless there is contact, we cannot take action from our side.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ない限り — as long as ... does not / unless

facts.quizQuestion:
奇跡が起き{___}、あのチームには勝てない。
(Unless a miracle happens, we can't beat that team.)
facts.explanation: ない限り (unless) — 奇跡が起きない限り、あのチームには勝てない. Sets an extreme condition as the only path to the outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kiseki ga oki ___ , ano chiimu ni ha kate nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Unless a miracle happens, we can't beat that team.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ない限り — unless

facts.quizQuestion:
許可が下り{___}、工事は始められない。
(Unless permission is granted, construction cannot begin.)
facts.explanation: ない限り (unless) — 許可が下りない限り、工事は始められない. Legal or procedural prerequisite stated as an absolute.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyoka ga kudari ___ , kouji ha hajime rare nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Unless permission is granted, construction cannot begin.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ない限り — unless

facts.quizQuestion:
自分で確認し{___}、信じることはできない。
(Unless I confirm it myself, I cannot believe it.)
facts.explanation: ない限り (unless) — 自分で確認しない限り、信じることはできない. Personal verification as the only acceptable standard.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: jibun de kakunin shi ___ , shinjiru koto ha deki nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Unless I confirm it myself, I cannot believe it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ない限り — unless

facts.quizQuestion:
完璧では{___}、十分な出来だと思う。
(Even if it is not perfect, I think it is a sufficient result.)
facts.explanation: ないまでも (even if not/at least) — 完璧ではないまでも、十分な出来だと思う。Accepts less than the ideal while still being positive.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanpeki de ha ___ , juubun na deki da to omou
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if it is not perfect, I think it is a sufficient result.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないまでも — even if not/at least

facts.quizQuestion:
毎日では{___}、週に一度は連絡してほしい。
(Even if not every day, I would like you to contact me at least once a week.)
facts.explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — 毎日ではないまでも、週に一度は連絡してほしい。Sets a lower acceptable threshold than the ideal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: mainichi de ha ___ , shuu ni ichi do ha renraku shi te hoshii
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if not every day, I would like you to contact me at least once a week.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないまでも — even if not

facts.quizQuestion:
賞をとら{___}、入賞ぐらいはしたい。
(Even if I don't win a prize, I at least want to place.)
facts.explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — 賞をとらないまでも、入賞ぐらいはしたい。Expresses a realistic, scaled-down goal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: shou wo tora ___ , nyuushou gurai ha shi tai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if I don't win a prize, I at least want to place.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないまでも — even if not

facts.quizQuestion:
トップには立て{___}、上位には入りたい。
(Even if I can't reach the top, I want to be in the upper rankings.)
facts.explanation: ないまでも (even if not) — トップには立てないまでも、上位には入りたい。Acknowledges a limit while still aspiring to a worthy goal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: toppu ni ha tate ___ , joui ni ha hairi tai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if I can't reach the top, I want to be in the upper rankings.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないまでも — even if not

facts.quizQuestion:
明日、雨が降ら{___}から、傘を持っていこう。
(Since we can't rule out rain tomorrow, let's take an umbrella.)
facts.explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 雨が降らないとも限らないから、傘を持っていこう. Hedges against a possibility that cannot be excluded.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to fall (of rain, snow, ash, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: ashita, ame ga fura ___ kara, kasa wo mo te iko u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since we can't rule out rain tomorrow, let's take an umbrella.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないとも限らない — can't rule out

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が嘘をついてい{___}ので、注意したほうがいい。
(Since we can't rule out that he is lying, it's better to be careful.)
facts.explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 嘘をついていないとも限らないので、注意したほうがいい. Advises caution based on an unverifiable but possible situation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: to take (seat, position, course, office, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga uso wo tsui te i ___ node, chuui shi ta hou ga ii
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since we can't rule out that he is lying, it's better to be careful.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないとも限らない — can't rule out

facts.quizQuestion:
成功し{___}から、準備をしっかりしよう。
(Since we can't rule out success, let's prepare thoroughly.)
facts.explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — 成功しないとも限らないから、準備をしっかりしよう. Encourages preparation by allowing for a positive outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: seikou shi ___ kara, junbi wo shikkari shiyo u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since we can't rule out success, let's prepare thoroughly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないとも限らない — can't rule out

facts.quizQuestion:
また会え{___}ので、今は別れを惜しもう。
(Since we can't rule out seeing each other again, let's savour this parting.)
facts.explanation: ないとも限らない (can't rule out) — また会えないとも限らないので、今は別れを惜しもう. Bittersweet acknowledgement of uncertainty.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: mata ae ___ node, ima ha wakare wo oshimo u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since we can't rule out seeing each other again, let's savour this parting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ないとも限らない — can't rule out

facts.quizQuestion:
今から急い{___}、電車には間に合わない。
(Even if I hurry now, I won't make the train.)
facts.explanation: たところで (even if/supposing) — 今から急いだところで、電車には間に合わない。The hypothetical action is futile.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ima kara isoi ___ , densha ni ha maniawa nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if I hurry now, I won't make the train.
facts.grammarPointLabel: たところで — even if/supposing

facts.quizQuestion:
謝っ{___}、許してもらえるとは限らない。
(Even if you apologise, it doesn't mean you will be forgiven.)
facts.explanation: たところで (even if) — 謝ったところで、許してもらえるとは限らない。Cautions that the assumed positive effect may not follow.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ayama ___ , yurushi te moraeru to ha kagira nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if you apologise, it doesn't mean you will be forgiven.
facts.grammarPointLabel: たところで — even if

facts.quizQuestion:
一人で考え{___}、答えは出ないだろう。
(Even if you think about it alone, you probably won't find the answer.)
facts.explanation: たところで (even if) — 一人で考えたところで、答えは出ないだろう。Suggests the approach is insufficient for the task.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: ichi nin de kangae ___ , kotae ha de nai daro u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if you think about it alone, you probably won't find the answer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: たところで — even if

facts.quizQuestion:
もし成功し{___}、それだけ大きなリスクも伴う。
(Even if one were to succeed, it comes with equally great risk.)
facts.explanation: たところで (even if/supposing) — 成功したところで、それだけ大きなリスクも伴う. Acknowledges a positive outcome while tempering enthusiasm.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: moshi seikou shi ___ , soredake ookina risuku mo tomonau
facts.sentenceTranslation: Even if one were to succeed, it comes with equally great risk.
facts.grammarPointLabel: たところで — even if/supposing

facts.quizQuestion:
家族のため{___}、どんな苦労もいとわない。
(If it is for my family, I will not mind any hardship.)
facts.explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 家族のためとあれば、どんな苦労もいとわない. The condition is taken as sufficient justification for any sacrifice.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kazoku no tame ___ , donna kurou mo itowa nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: If it is for my family, I will not mind any hardship.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とあれば — if that is the case

facts.quizQuestion:
必要{___}、すぐに駆けつけます。
(If it is necessary, I will come right away.)
facts.explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 必要とあれば、すぐに駆けつけます. Expresses unconditional willingness given a specific trigger.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: hitsuyou ___ , sugu ni kaketsuke masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: If it is necessary, I will come right away.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とあれば — if that is the case

facts.quizQuestion:
君の頼み{___}、断れないよ。
(If it is your request, I can't say no.)
facts.explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 君の頼みとあれば、断れないよ. Personal relationship makes refusal impossible.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kimi no tanomi ___ , kotoware nai yo
facts.sentenceTranslation: If it is your request, I can't say no.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とあれば — if that is the case

facts.quizQuestion:
国のため{___}、命を捧げる覚悟がある。
(If it is for the country, I am prepared to give my life.)
facts.explanation: とあれば (if that is the case) — 国のためとあれば、命を捧げる覚悟がある. Extreme commitment justified by a cause.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kuni no tame ___ , inochi wo sasageru kakugo ga aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: If it is for the country, I am prepared to give my life.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とあれば — if that is the case

facts.quizQuestion:
医者{___}、そんな無責任なことを言うとは。
(For someone who is supposed to be a doctor, saying such an irresponsible thing.)
facts.explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 医者ともあろうものが、そんな無責任なことを言うとは。Strongly criticises someone who fails to meet the standards of their position.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: isha ___ , sonna musekinin na koto wo iu to ha
facts.sentenceTranslation: For someone who is supposed to be a doctor, saying such an irresponsible thing.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともあろうものが — for someone of that standing

facts.quizQuestion:
教師{___}、生徒にそんな言葉を使うとは信じられない。
(I can't believe someone who is supposed to be a teacher would use such words to a student.)
facts.explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 教師ともあろうものが、生徒にそんな言葉を使うとは。Expresses shock at conduct unworthy of the stated role.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyoushi ___ , seito ni sonna kotoba wo tsukau to ha shinji rare nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: I can't believe someone who is supposed to be a teacher would use such words to a student.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともあろうものが — for someone of that standing

facts.quizQuestion:
チャンピオン{___}、一回戦で負けるとは。
(For someone who is supposed to be a champion to lose in the first round.)
facts.explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — チャンピオンともあろうものが、一回戦で負けるとは。Expresses disbelief at an outcome that belies the person's status.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: event with ... rounds, innings, legs, etc.

facts.sentenceRomaji: chanpion ___ , ichi kaisen de makeru to ha
facts.sentenceTranslation: For someone who is supposed to be a champion to lose in the first round.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともあろうものが — for someone of that standing

facts.quizQuestion:
議員{___}、収賄に関わるとはあきれた話だ。
(For someone who is supposed to be a legislator to be involved in bribery is astounding.)
facts.explanation: ともあろうものが (for someone of that standing) — 議員ともあろうものが、収賄に関わるとは。Strong moral condemnation of a person in authority.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: giin ___ , shuuwai ni kakawaru to ha akire ta hanashi da
facts.sentenceTranslation: For someone who is supposed to be a legislator to be involved in bribery is astounding.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともあろうものが — for someone of that standing

facts.quizQuestion:
社長{___}、責任も大きくなる。
(When it comes to being president, responsibility also becomes great.)
facts.explanation: ともなると (when it comes to/at that level) — 社長ともなると、責任も大きくなる。Used when a high-status role brings with it expected demands.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: shachou ___ , sekinin mo ookiku naru
facts.sentenceTranslation: When it comes to being president, responsibility also becomes great.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともなると — when it comes to/at that level

facts.quizQuestion:
大学受験{___}、家族全員が緊張する。
(When it comes to university entrance exams, the whole family gets nervous.)
facts.explanation: ともなると (when it comes to that) — 大学受験ともなると、家族全員が緊張する。Highlights how a significant event affects those around it.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

sentenceFurigana.g: taking an examination (esp. for entrance to a school or university)

facts.sentenceRomaji: daigaku juken ___ , kazoku zen'in ga kinchou suru
facts.sentenceTranslation: When it comes to university entrance exams, the whole family gets nervous.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともなると — when it comes to that

facts.quizQuestion:
親{___}、子供の将来を真剣に考えるものだ。
(When one becomes a parent, one naturally thinks seriously about a child's future.)
facts.explanation: ともなると (when it comes to being) — 親ともなると、子供の将来を真剣に考えるものだ。Used when reaching a role entails natural responsibilities.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: oya ___ , kodomo no shourai wo shinken ni kangaeru mono da
facts.sentenceTranslation: When one becomes a parent, one naturally thinks seriously about a child's future.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともなると — when it comes to being

facts.quizQuestion:
プロ選手{___}、練習の質も変わってくる。
(When it comes to being a professional athlete, the quality of practice also changes.)
facts.explanation: ともなると (when it comes to that level) — プロ選手ともなると、練習の質も変わってくる。Marks the threshold at which expectations shift.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: puro senshu ___ , renshuu no shitsu mo kawa te kuru
facts.sentenceTranslation: When it comes to being a professional athlete, the quality of practice also changes.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ともなると — when it comes to that level

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が参加{___}、会議は開催される。
(Whether or not he participates, the meeting will be held.)
facts.explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 彼が参加しようがしまいが、会議は開催される。Both possibilities are dismissed as irrelevant to the outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ga sanka ___ , kaigi ha kaisai sa reru
facts.sentenceTranslation: Whether or not he participates, the meeting will be held.
facts.grammarPointLabel: (よ)うが～まいが — whether or not

facts.quizQuestion:
君が{___}、私は関係ない。
(Whether you go or not, it has nothing to do with me.)
facts.explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 君が行こうが行くまいが、私は関係ない。Speaker declares complete indifference to the outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kimi ga ___ , watashi ha kankei nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Whether you go or not, it has nothing to do with me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: (よ)うが～まいが — whether or not

facts.correctAnswer: 賛成しようがしまいが
facts.quizQuestion:
みんなが{___}、この計画は進む。
(Whether everyone agrees or not, this plan will proceed.)
facts.explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — みんなが賛成しようがしまいが、この計画は進む。Strong assertion that consensus is irrelevant.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.reading: さんせいしようがしまいが
facts.sentenceRomaji: minna ga ___ , kono keikaku ha susumu
facts.sentenceTranslation: Whether everyone agrees or not, this plan will proceed.
facts.grammarPointLabel: (よ)うが～まいが — whether or not

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女が{___}、パーティーは楽しくなるだろう。
(Whether she comes or not, the party will likely be fun.)
facts.explanation: (よ)うが～まいが (whether or not) — 彼女が来ようが来まいが、パーティーは楽しくなるだろう。Outcome is asserted as independent of the person's choice.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ga ___ , paateii ha tanoshiku naru daro u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Whether she comes or not, the party will likely be fun.
facts.grammarPointLabel: (よ)うが～まいが — whether or not

facts.correctAnswer: どう言おうが何を言おうが
facts.quizQuestion:
{___}、私の気持ちは変わらない。
(No matter what you say or how you say it, my feelings won't change.)
facts.explanation: ようが～ようが (whether ... or, no matter) — どう言おうが何を言おうが、私の気持ちは変わらない. Two parallel volitional forms dismiss all variants of the condition.

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarPointLabel: に耐える — worthy of / able to withstand

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.targetLanguageWord: に限ったことではない
facts.reading: にかぎったことではない
facts.pronunciation: ni kagitta koto de wa nai
facts.sentenceRomaji: kodoku wo kanjiru no ha hitorigurashi no hito ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: Feeling lonely is not limited to people living alone.

facts.quizQuestion:
これは命{___}重大な問題だ。
(This is a serious matter related to one's life.)
facts.explanation: にかかわる (related to/affecting) — 命にかかわる重大な問題だ。Often used with weighty subjects like life, honor, reputation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kore ha inochi ___ juudai na mondai da
facts.sentenceTranslation: This is a serious matter related to one's life.
facts.grammarPointLabel: にかかわる — related to/affecting

facts.quizQuestion:
これは会社の名誉{___}スキャンダルだ。
(This is a scandal affecting the company's honor.)
facts.explanation: にかかわる (affecting/related to) — 名誉にかかわるスキャンダルだ。Implies serious consequences.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: kore ha kaisha no meiyo ___ sukyandaru da
facts.sentenceTranslation: This is a scandal affecting the company's honor.
facts.grammarPointLabel: にかかわる — affecting/related to

facts.quizQuestion:
安全{___}規制は絶対に守らなければならない。
(Safety-related regulations must absolutely be followed.)
facts.explanation: にかかわる (related to) — 安全にかかわる規制は守らなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: anzen ___ kisei ha zettai ni mamora nakere ba nara nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Safety-related regulations must absolutely be followed.
facts.grammarPointLabel: にかかわる — related to

facts.quizQuestion:
それは国家の存亡{___}問題だ。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.sentenceRomaji: sore ha kokka no sonbou ___ mondai da
facts.sentenceTranslation: That is a matter affecting the survival of the nation.

facts.quizQuestion:
この問題は日本{___}、世界中で議論されている。

facts.explanation: のみならず (not only A but also B) — 日本のみならず、世界中で議論されている。Formal equivalent of だけでなく.

facts.sentenceRomaji: kono mondai ha nippon ___ , sekaijuu de giron sa re te iru

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha eigo ___ , furansugo mo ryuuchou ni hanasu
facts.sentenceTranslation: He speaks not only English but also French fluently.

facts.quizQuestion:
その薬は効果がある{___}、副作用も少ない。

facts.quizQuestion:
ただ前に進む{___}だ。

ただ結果を待つ{___}だった。

ただ彼の無事を祈る{___}だ。

その時の私にはただ涙を流す{___}だった。

facts.sentenceRomaji: aji ___ mitame ___ , kono ryouri ha kanpeki da
facts.sentenceTranslation: Both in flavor and appearance, this dish is perfect.

facts.quizQuestion:
性格{___}実力{___}、彼は申し分ない候補者だ。

facts.quizQuestion:
立地{___}設備{___}、このホテルは最高だ。

facts.quizQuestion:
デザイン{___}使いやすさ{___}、この製品はよくできている。

facts.quizQuestion:
平日{___}休日{___}、彼は毎日練習した。

facts.sentenceTranslation: Whether on weekdays or weekends, he practiced every day.

facts.quizQuestion:
昼{___}夜{___}、工事の音が続いた。

facts.explanation: といわず～といわず (whether A or B) — 昼といわず夜といわず、工事の音が続いた。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
春{___}秋{___}、この公園には観光客が絶えない。

facts.sentenceTranslation: All of his works are wonderful, but I especially like this painting.

facts.quizQuestion:
今年の夏は暑かったが、{___}8月が耐えがたいほどだった。

facts.quizQuestion:
子供たちは外遊びが好きだが、{___}サッカーに夢中だ。

sentenceFurigana.g: long sword (as opposed to the shorter katana)

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha supootsu ga tokui da ga, ___ suiei de ha zenkoku taikai ni shutsujou shi ta

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
二人はただの友達{___}。

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の行動は特別なこと{___}、普通のことだ。

facts.quizQuestion:
